problem: ZIP metadata was nondeterministic and source traversal followed symlinks.
mathematical_or_language_reason: ZIP bytes depend on metadata; is_file follows symlinks.
failure_example: Two builds can hash differently or include an external target.
fix: Fixed metadata, regular-file bounds, atomic build, external sidecar and double-build validation.

problem: User-controlled --target could be recursively deleted by --force or --uninstall.
mathematical_or_language_reason: Destruction lacked ownership and boundary invariants.
fix: Managed marker, dangerous-target rejection, staging, unique backup and rollback.

problem: Second-resolution project IDs could collide and initialization was non-atomic.
mathematical_or_language_reason: A one-second timestamp is not injective.
failure_example: Two projects initialized in one second share an ID.
fix: UTC plus UUID ID and staged replacement.

problem: Claim–Evidence references were only checked in one direction.
failure_example: CL-1 lists EV-1 while EV-1 omits CL-1.
fix: Bidirectional links, duplicate checks and support/refute disjointness.

problem: Substring matching, duplicate keywords and reverse lexical ties produced unstable routing.
mathematical_or_language_reason: Scores changed under duplicate rules and ties ignored declaration order.
fix: NFKC/casefold, word boundaries, deduplication, bounds, cache and stable ties.

problem: Mutable Action tags and narrow CI omitted major gates.
mathematical_or_language_reason: Tags are not content-addressed and one platform is not cross-platform evidence.
failure_example: actions/checkout@v4 can change without a repository diff.
fix: Pinned SHAs, read-only permissions, timeouts, 3-OS/4-Python matrix and quality gates.

problem: NaN/Infinity and non-atomic writes were accepted.
mathematical_or_language_reason: Non-finite values are outside standard JSON and partial writes violate atomicity.
fix: Finite JSON, strict UTF-8, bounds, fsync/replace and symlink guards.

problem: No bounded safe ZIP intake existed.
failure_example: ../escape, absolute paths, links and compression bombs.
risk: Zip Slip and denial of service.
fix: Bounded validation and atomic manual extraction.

problem: Temporal, approval and idempotency invariants were incomplete.
mathematical_or_language_reason: Schema membership alone is not a state-machine proof.
failure_example: accepted without approval or updated_at before created_at.
fix: Transition, timestamp, rationale and approval gates.

problem: Schemas lacked stable IDs and a capability contract.
failure_example: Duplicate IDs or extra fields accepted.
rollback: Version schemas instead of loosening silently.

problem: Tests were mainly positive existence checks.
mathematical_or_language_reason: They did not distinguish correct code from critical mutants.
fix: Negative, property, adversarial, deterministic and mutation tests.

problem: Child processes had no timeout or explicit shell policy.
failure_example: Hung validation occupies a runner indefinitely.
fix: Argument vectors, shell=false, controlled environment and timeouts.
rollback: Raise timeout rather than remove it.

problem: errors=ignore and weak workflow scanning could hide opaque source.
mathematical_or_language_reason: Ignoring undecodable bytes makes scanning non-total.
fix: Strict inventory, symlink, pinned Action, bootstrap, secret and shell checks.

problem: Dependencies lacked upper bounds and quality tools.
mathematical_or_language_reason: Future major resolution is not reproducible.
failure_example: pytest>=8 resolves to an incompatible major.
fix: Compatible upper bounds and explicit tooling.
